reason: Could not refute. The change neither under-flags a real defect class nor lets a known-bad fixture slip. VERIFIED: (1) The bad fixture (good/env-sourced-secret is clean; bad/hardcoded-secret) keeps its discriminating value -- bad/hardcoded-secret plants a literal default api_secret_key: str = "super-secret-key-do-not-share", an actual committed secret VALUE on a changed line, which the rewrite's 'bind secret-exposing to an ACTUAL committed/leaked secret VALUE' clause still fires on (critical 3/3 in baseline); no fixture edit touches the bad case, so it stays unambiguous. (2) The good fixture is genuinely clean, not a dirty-clean: api_secret_key: str (env-sourced, no default) byte-mirrors the template idiom webhook_signing_secret: str = "" in template/config/settings.py.jinja -- grep confirms ZERO SecretStr/get_secret_value/min_length anywhere in template/. So the baseline fp 1.00 (r0: medium SecretStr + low min_length; r1: HIGH SecretStr + medium + low env_file; r2: medium + low lru_cache-rotation + info naming) is 100% over-flag of hardening the codebase itself omits, plus out-of-diff reaching (lru_cache/env_file are not on changed lines). (3) Root cause is the prompt, confirmed: current security.md says 'critical/high = exploitable or secret-exposing' with no clause tying 'secret-exposing' to a leaked value and no codebase-bar, so the model reads SecretStr-absence as exposure; the rewrite supplies exactly that missing clause + forbids hardening-as-high + enforces added/modified-lines-only review, which also fixes the r1 SecretStr-high escalation a threshold change alone would not. (4) Scope is preserved: OWASP/authz/injection/CVE coverage is untouched; only SecretStr/min_length/rotation/.env are demoted to info-at-most (rubric-aligned: .env is already gitignored). (5) The needs_second_good_fixture=true add is conservative coverage-widening, and threshold-rederivation-001 explicitly forbids widening fp_max to mask the over-flag (re-derived Phase 3). No loosened severity/threshold lets the bad fixture through; the good fixture is verifiably clean against the template idiom.
